relative_path: Inventories - Reagents, samples, equipment/ASA registry/ASA follow-up.xlsx
full_text:
R1: Follow-up for the exposure of carcinogenic chemicals and working methos
R3: Exposure of substances classified as carcinogenic (Carc. 1A/1B; H350 or H350i) or mutagenic (Muta. 1A/1B; H340) must be reported to the ASA register.
R4: If the chemicals that you are using have following hazard staments, please record the exposure.
R5: H340 | May cause genetic defects
R6: H350 | May cause cancer
R7: H350i | May cause cancer when inhaled
R9: Fill in the information for each chemical into different table in worksheet User1.
R11: To make it easier to remember to record the exposure, it would be recommended to mark carcinogenic substances e.g. with sticker that says | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ASA | or use the pictogram GHS08
R13: Some of the substances have been given a so-called harmonized classification by the EU. The harmonized classification can be checked from the database of the European Chemicals Agency:
R14: https://echa.europa.eu/information-on-chemicals/cl-inventory-database
R16: Also following working methods require recording:
R17: Work involving exposure to crystalline silicon dioxide (quartz dust)
R18: Work involving exposure to hardwood dust, i.e. deciduous wood dust
R19: Work involving exposure to used motor oils
R20: Work involving exposure to diesel engine exhaust
R21: Welding and flame cutting stainless steel

dust, i.e. deciduous wood dust
R19: Work involving exposure to used motor oils
R20: Work involving exposure to diesel engine exhaust
R21: Welding and flame cutting stainless steel
R22: Work involving exposure to carcinogenic substances created in the combustion process
R23: Work involving exposure to carcinogenic cytoctatics with anatomical therapeutic chemical (ATC) classification
R25: The employer’s obligation to maintain a list and report to the ASA register applies both to substances given a harmonized classification
R26: and to substances classified by each manufacturer (so-called self-classified substances).
R27: Self-classified substances do not have a harmonized, EU-level classification, which is why there may be variation in the classifications of different manufacturers.
R28: Reports to the ASA register are always made according to the classification of the specific product used at the workplace.
R29: Check the classification of a substance or a product from its safety data sheet.
R31: More detailed information at the Finnish Institute of Occupational Health webpages:
R32: https://www.ttl.fi/en/specification-of-carcinogenic-substances-requiring-asa-registration-to-be-amended-as-of-1-january-2020/
